content.full_text:
Re
Fern Polrrrcel PnecrrcEs CouurssroN
428 J Street r Suite 620 o Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 r Fax (916) 322-0886
March 7,2006
Diane Guyon
El Dorado County
Recorder-Clerk
Registrar of Voters
360 Fair Lane
Placerville,CA 95667
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. 4-05-214
Dear Ms. Guyon
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the campaign
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").I The Commission's advicL and
assistance is limited to the provisions of the Act. Other provisions of the law, such as the
California Public Records Act, (Cal. Gov. Code $$ 6250-6276.49),may have some
bearing on your situation.
QUESTION
When the El Dorado County Recorder-Clerk Registrar of Voters receives a
request for campaign statements to be mailed or faxed, can the fees associated with
mailing or faxing be passed on to the requestor, in addition to the rate of ten cents per
page for copying?
CONCLUSION
Section 81008, which is the provision goveming the handling of reports and
statements filed under the Act, does not specifically provide for, or authorize, the
charging of an additional fee for postage andlor faxing. The statute provides that fees
may not exceed the copy charge of ten cents per page and the retrieval charge of five
dollars per request for documents which are five or more years old.
I Govemment Code sections 81000 - 9l}l4. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
qS


File No. 05-214
Page No. 2
FACTS
The El Dorado County Recorder-Clerk Registrar of Voters is the filing officer for
campaign statements filed in El Dorado County. As filing officer, the El Dorado County
Recorder-Clerk Registrar of Voters is required to retain the campaign statements for a
period of not less than four years and in some circumstances for an indefinite period.
These copies are available for the public to freely view in your office. If a member of the
public wishes to purchase copies, you charge a fee of ten cents per page.
You have received requests for these campaignstatements to be mailed or faxed
to members of the public. You inquire as to the legality of charging an additional fee to
cover the costs of mailing or faxing the documents.
rhe purpose orthe Potitical ."#:::::" 
"r.*" that campaign staternents as
well as other political records are fully and truthfully disclosed so thai the public may be
fully informed and improper practices may be inhibited. (Section s 1002.)
Section 81008 is a specific provision governing the handling of reports and
statements filed under the Act. It states:
"Every report and statement filed pursuant to this title is a
public record open for public inspection and reproduction
during regular business hours... No conditions whatsoever
shall be imposed upon persons desiring to inspect or
reproduce reports and statements filed under this title, nor
shall any information or identification be required from
these persons. copies shalr be provided at a chargenot to
exceed ten cents ($0.10) per page. In addition, filing
officers are permitted to charge a retrieval fee not to exceed
five dollars ($5) per request for copies of reports and
statements which are five or more years old. A request for
more than one report or statement or report and statement at
the same time shall be considered a single request.,,
The El Dorado County Recorder-Clerk Registrar of Voters is the filing officer for
campaign statements filed in El Dorado County. (Section 82027.) Accordingl% you may
charge a fee of not more than ten cents ($0.10) per page and,if applicable, fiiedollars
per request for copies of campaign statements. This fee may be charged regardless of the
method chosen for transmittal of these documents. For example, yo,, -uy Jhurg" this fee
when you print the documents for the purpose of faxing therrrto the requistor.2
2 Section 81007.5 imposes the same requirements on faxed statements or reports.


File No. 05-214
Page No. 3
Section 81008 does not address whether a fee may be charged for mailing a
statement or report. However, we note that section 81008 imposes a duty on filing
officers to "provide" copies of reports and statements filed pursuant to the Act. The
statute also states that'ono conditions whatsoever shall be imposed upon persons desiring
to inspect or reproduce reports and statements filed under the Act." (Section g 100g(a),
emphasis added.) The strong language used within section 81008 demonstrates the
Iegislative intent to make these copies readily accessible to the general pubtic. (North
county Parents organization v. Department of Education e99$ n cA. App.'4h 144,
152 citing CBS, Inc. v. Block (1986) 42 Cal.3d 646, 651-652.) Therefore, if you provide
public records via ordinary mall atno charge, it would be in furtherance of tire pu{poses
of section 81008 to mail statements and reports at no cost.
However, if the practice of the El Dorado County Recorder-Clerk Registrar of
Voters is to charge mailing fees in the case of an extraordinary request for a means of
transmittal that the agerrcy normally does not provide to the general public under the
Public Records Act -such 
as overnight or express mail fees in connection with an
expedited request - 
we would not construe Section 81008 so broadly as to require
provision of statements by these extraordinary means.
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
By: Emelyn Rodriguez
Counsel, Legal Division
ER:rd

When the El Dorado County Recorder-Clerk Registrar of Voters receives a
request for campaign statements to be mailed or faxed, can the fees associated with
mailing or faxing be passed on to the requestor, in addition to the rate of ten cents per
page for copying?
sections.conclusion:
Section 81008, which is the provision goveming the handling of reports and
statements filed under the Act, does not specifically provide for, or authorize, the
charging of an additional fee for postage andlor faxing. The statute provides that fees
may not exceed the copy charge of ten cents per page and the retrieval charge of five
dollars per request for documents which are five or more years old.
qS

request for campaign statements to be mailed or faxed, can the fees associated with
